title: Question about Two Songs
wikitext:
[]: Question about Two Songs             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Sep 16, 2011 12:13 am So I was listening to some Eva music I had, and I came across two songs that I didn't recognize. Well, one that I didn't, and one that I did but I had a question. I checked the Wiki and couldn't find anything about either, so I thought I'd ask here. The first is called "The Tempest" and it's like techno or something. I can't tell if it's a techno remix of a song from Eva, but I can't find it on anything about it on the wiki, or through a quick google/youtube search. Does anyone know where it's from, or perhaps if it's been mislabeled and isn't from Eva at all? The second is labeled "Cruel Angel's Thesis (San Nin)". It's singer is labeled as Megumi Hayashibara who I know is the voice of Rei, but I swear I can hear Asuka and perhaps Misato in there. I'm simply curious if I'm right and what album (Or whatever) this is from. Sorry if I've missed something obvious, but I just couldn't find anything so I thought someone hear would know more than me.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Sep 16, 2011 12:45 am The second song you're referring to I've never heard of under that name, but such a version of Cruel Angel's Thesis does exist, it's the first track on NGE Addition (the CD that includes After the End) and I think it's on Birthday of Ayanami Rei or Evangelion Decade or something as well? Never heard of the first one.

[]: Re: Question about Two Songs             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Sep 16, 2011 8:09 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 16, 2011 8:23 am I have a low-rez copy of "Cruel Angel's Thesis (San Nin)" I found on the internet, and would love to know which album you found that in so I could just buy the hard copy. I do believe it's the vocal talents for Rei, Asuka, and Misato singing. One of the more interesting thing to realize is who is signing which lyric as they trade off the song with each other, and how perfect/wrong it is. Rei: A blue wind is now knocking at the door to your heart, Asuka: ...and yet you are merely gazing at me and smiling. Misato: Something gently touching-- you're so intent on seeking it out, Rei: ...that you can't even see your fate yet, ALL: ...with such innocent eyes. The whole thing not only seems to sound like they're singing about the on-screen images (Asuka's first line syncs with Shinji smiling after her upside-down silhouette fades away,) but also eems to remind me of that moment in EoE where all three ladies were talking to Shinji on the Hell Train during Third Impacts. What's even more interesting is when you isolate the lyrics to the voice singing them: Rei: A blue wind is now knocking at the door to your heart, ...that you can't even see your fate yet, ...with such innocent eyes. Asuka: You are merely gazing at me and smiling, ...with such innocent eyes. Misato: Something gently touching-- you're so intent on seeking it out, ...with such innocent eyes.

[]: Something belched out of the Internets. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Sep 16, 2011 8:53 am CJD wrote: The first is called "The Tempest" and it's like techno or something. I can't tell if it's a techno remix of a song from Eva, but I can't find it on anything about it on the wiki, or through a quick google/youtube search. Does anyone know where it's from, or perhaps if it's been mislabeled and isn't from Eva at all?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Sep 16, 2011 9:14 am Thanks to everyone, that answered my questions! FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I have a low-rez copy of "Cruel Angel's Thesis (San Nin)" I found on the internet, and would love to know which album you found that in so I could just buy the hard copy.

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Fri Sep 16, 2011 7:03 pm The three of them ( Kotono Mitsuishi, Megumi Hayashibara and Yuko Miyamura ) also performed "Fly me to the moon", always in Addition ( and in Decade ). Bye

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Sep 16, 2011 7:54 pm More about the seiyuu versions of the OP and ED can be found in this old thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7040

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 16, 2011 8:07 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: More about the seiyuu versions of the OP and ED can be found in this old thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7040

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Sep 16, 2011 10:10 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: More about the seiyuu versions of the OP and ED can be found in this old thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7040
